title: Arctic Wolf and BlackBerry Complete Cylance Acquisition
text: Arctic Wolf and BlackBerry announced the successful closing of the acquisition of BlackBerry's Cylance endpoint security assets by Arctic Wolf on February 3, 2025. The two companies entered into a definitive agreement on December 15, 2024. BlackBerry will sell its Cylance assets to Arctic Wolf for $160 million of cash, subject to certain adjustments, and approximately 5.5 million common shares of Arctic Wolf. After allowing for purchase price adjustments, BlackBerry will receive approximately $80 million of cash at closing and approximately $40 million of cash one year following the closing. By integrating Cylance's advanced AI-driven capabilities into the Aurora Platform, combined with the expertise of Arctic Wolf's security operations centers, Aurora Endpoint Security offerings help organizations reduce risk exposure with better prevention and threat detection. BlackBerry will continue its relationship with Arctic Wolf as a customer, as a reseller of the portfolio to its large government customers, and as a shareholder of the company. This transaction allowed BlackBerry to focus on its core QNX and Secure Communications businesses.
